$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports verbatim.

TypeScript port. Omits the blob-URL upload path (useUploadMediaFromBlobURL), the clipboard toolbar button (useCopyToClipboard), and the PDF preview ResizableBox + PDF inspector panel — all depend on block-library internals not exposed via the package's exports field. The inspector link-destination/open-in-new-tab/show-download-button ToolsPanel is inlined directly; useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps and removeAnchorTag are inlined as local helpers.
TypeScript port; behaviour byte-equivalent to upstream.
All three deprecation entries (v3, v2, v1) ported verbatim.
Upstream files + cross-media block transforms preserved; added bidirectional core/file ↔ artisanpack/file block transforms.
Sass @use base-styles/colors inlined as literal #fff (only $white was referenced). Nested rules flattened to plain CSS.
Inline SVG for the inserter (matches @wordpress/icons file path).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; upstream init.js / index.php server-side wiring is intentionally absent.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx omits the upstream blob-URL upload mount-effect (useUploadMediaFromBlobURL). Practical impact: drag-and-dropped files are not eagerly uploaded as soon as they hit the block; the user uploads via MediaPlaceholder or MediaReplaceFlow as usual.

edit.tsx omits the upstream ClipboardToolbarButton (depends on @wordpress/compose useCopyToClipboard). Practical impact: the 'Copy URL' toolbar button is not present; users copy the file URL via the inspector or the rendered <a href> directly.

edit.tsx omits the PDF preview ResizableBox and the PDF settings ToolsPanel (show inline embed / height in pixels). The displayPreview and previewHeight attributes are still saved verbatim — only the editor-side preview surface is simplified to a non-resizable rendering.

edit.tsx inlines the FileBlockInspector ToolsPanel directly and replaces useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps + removeAnchorTag with local equivalents so the fork doesn't depend on block-library internals not exposed via the package's exports.
